content.full_text:
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
James Murdoch 
Admlnistratian •• 
Executive/legal 
•• 
Enfarcement 
322-5660 
322·5901 
322-64.41 
January 5, 1982 
Murdoch, Mockler & Associates 
1010 Eleventh Street, Suite 304 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Murdoch: 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Thank you for your telephone call requesting advice from 
this office. 
The facts, as you presented them to me, are that 
you are currently an attorney and a registered lobbyist. 
In the 
past, you ~ave worked as a legislative consultant for Assembly-
person Leroy Greene. 
While working in the latter capacity, you 
formed a partnership with another legislative employee, a person 
who later took over the position you vacated. 
The partnership owns a parcel of pr9perty for which your 
partner keeps the books. 
Due to the fact that this investment 
has a negative cash flow, you have, in the past, written personal 
checks to your partner to cover your share of the expenses. 
Your 
question is whether, as a registered lobbyist, you must disclose 
these checks under Government Code Section 86107. 
Section 86l07(b) requires disclosure of "expenses which 
benefit in whole or in part any ••• official •••• " 
Under the 
facts you presented to me, it appears that you are merely providing 
your partner with money to cover your share of the property's 
expenses and are not conferring any benefit upon him. 
If, however, 
you were to pay a portion of his share of the partnership's expenses, 
then you would be providing him with a benefit which would have 
to be disclosed. 
It should also be noted that it appears that these payments 
are not income to your partner nor are they gifts. 
(Section 
86l07(b) and 86203.) 
If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact 
me at 916/322-5901. 
JSM:km 
Very tru~y~~~ 
~nk 
McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Under the facts presented, it appears that you are merely providing your partner with money to cover your share of the property's expenses and are not conferring any benefit upon him. If, however, you were to pay a portion of his share of the partnership's expenses, then you would be providing him with a benefit which would have to be disclosed. It should also be noted that it appears that these payments are not income to your partner nor are they gifts.
sections.question_synthetic: A registered lobbyist who is part of a partnership owning property seeks advice on whether personal checks written to cover his share of partnership expenses must be disclosed under Government Code Section 86107.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that payments covering only the lobbyist's own share of partnership expenses do not require disclosure, as they confer no benefit on the partner; however, if the lobbyist paid any portion of his partner's share, such payments would require disclosure.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document from 1982. Clear question and conclusion sections present. Letter from Frank McLean, Counsel of Legal Division. Minor OCR artifacts noted (e.g., 'pr9perty', 'tru~y~~') but text is largely legible and complete.

these checks under Government Code Section 86107. 
Section 86l07(b) requires disclosure of "expenses which 
benefit in whole or in part any ••• official •••• " 
Under the 
facts you presented to me, it appears that you are merely providing 
your partner with money to cover your share of the property's 
expenses and are not conferring any benefit upon him. 
If, however, 
you were to pay a portion of his share of the partnership's expenses, 
then you would be providing him with a benefit which would have

It should also be noted that it appears that these payments

(Section 
86l07(b) and 86203.)

CONCLUSION: Under the facts presented, it appears that you are merely providing your partner with money to cover your share of the property's expenses and are not conferring any benefit upon him. If, however, you were to pay a portion of his share of the partnership's expenses, then you would be providing him with a benefit which would have to be disclosed. It should also be noted that it appears that these payments are not income to your partner nor are they gifts.

embedding.summary: FPPC counsel advises a registered lobbyist that personal checks written to a partner to cover the lobbyist's share of partnership property expenses do not require disclosure under Government Code Section 86107, unless the payments also cover the partner's share.
